body.title: Write /igor-diagnose skill — log-first structured stuck-state diagnosis
body.result: wrote /igor-diagnose skill at ~/.claude/skills/igor-diagnose/SKILL.md — 6-step protocol: anchor→leaf logs→anomaly→categorize(A/B/C)→fix→verify; appends incident entries to igor_diagnose_report.md
body.description:
Every time Igor gets stuck we diagnose from channel/TWM (effects), form a theory, and act. That's backwards — the cause is always in the logs, and it's always earlier than the stuck NE cycles. A skill encodes the discipline so CC can't skip the log-reading step.

**Skill name:** /igor-diagnose

**Core invariant:** No hypothesis before step 3 (first anomaly found in logs). This line is in the skill to block CC from theorizing.

**Protocol:**
1. Anchor — find timestamp of last healthy action in channel (last non-stuck substantive message)
2. Leaf log sweep — read Igor's specific subsystem logs from anchor time forward (NOT channel, NOT TWM). Leaf logs: ~/.TheIgors/logs/ hierarchy, pe_chain trace logs, goal/GOAL memory records. These are the authoritative record.
3. First anomaly — identify the exact log line where behavior diverged. State it verbatim. Stop here; do not proceed until the anomaly is in hand.
4. Categorize — three cases with different fixes:
   - Code/logic bug → fix in source
   - TWM/memory state corruption → DB cleanup, goal reset, or clarifying direction
   - External noise / false narrative loop → clarifying direction + optionally filter the source
5. One targeted fix — exactly what the logs point to, nothing more
6. Verify — watch for Igor to produce a successful action, not just absence of stuck messages

**Diagnostic report:** The skill appends each incident to a persistent report at ~/.TheIgors/claudecode/igor_diagnose_report.md. Format per entry: timestamp | trigger | leaf log citation | root cause category | fix applied | outcome. Over time this becomes a pattern library — the frequency distribution of failure modes drives what to fix in the system.

**Integration:** When leaf logs point to a pe_chain HYPOTHESIZE failure, escalate to /debug-pe-chain (that skill steps phases manually). /igor-diagnose is the triage layer; /debug-pe-chain is the deep-dive layer for one specific failure mode.

**Hard rules in the skill:**
- Leaf logs first, always — no diagnosis from channel or TWM alone
- No hypothesis before the verbatim anomaly is in hand
- If logs are ambiguous → add logging before guessing (per standing instruction: 'if there isn't enough logging, add more')
- Verify by watching Igor produce a successful action, not just silence

**Affected files:** ~/.claude/skills/igor-diagnose.md (new skill file)
**Design rules:** none (skill file only, no source changes)
**Scope boundary:** In scope: the skill file itself + the diagnostic report append logic. Out of scope: fixing any specific bug found — that's the repair action that follows the diagnosis.
**Test plan:** No automated tests — skill file is markdown. Manual verification: run /igor-diagnose on a known stuck incident from today's session and confirm the report entry captures the right leaf log citation and root cause.
